OF | Zebra Technologies | ## 1. OVERVIEW This document outlines specifications and standards for all cosmetic surface applications of Fetch Robotics. ## 2. COSMETIC STANDARDS #### **Cosmetic Class Requirements** All cosmetic finishes will be subject to Class "A" unless stated otherwise in Fetch Robotics engineering drawings, formal specifications, and other reference documents. #### **Surface Preparation:** - All surfaces must be cleaned to render them free of dust, grease, oil, rust, or other contamination. - All surface requiring Class A and B finish must be free of nicks, dents, scratches, metal dust and other tool marks before application of coating is applied - All holes and edges must be deburred using conventional methods to remove sharp edges. #### **Finish Specifications:** The total thickness of coating and substrate will be within the range of 0.003'' to 0.005''. #### **Inspection** Class A and Class B cosmetic surface finish shall be subject to a 100% inspection rate. #### **Time and Viewing Distance:** | Classification | Class A | Class B | | |---------------------|-----------|-----------|--| | Viewing
Time | 8 seconds | 2 seconds | | | Viewing
Distance | 24" | 30" | | To be viewed at the specified distance then rotated at 45 degrees for a 0-90 degree viewing angle. | • • | | | | |----------|----------------------------|-------|--------------| | Category | Surface Description | Class | Frequency | | I | External | Α | Usually View | | II | External | В | Seldom View | ![](_page_1_Picture_0.jpeg) Category **I** is exclusively used for all "critical" areas of clear window of visibility. Category **II** are "major" surface areas, also an indication of importance, but less so than class I, whereby surface may still be visible. ### **Class Defects and Acceptance**
